skill.name: amazon-best-selling-products-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured best-selling product data from Amazon via the BrowserAct API. Search products by keywords, get titles, prices, ratings, reviews, sales volume, and promotional details.
skill.summary: API-based Amazon product data extraction tool for market research and competitor analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill performs legitimate API integration with BrowserAct to extract Amazon product data. All static findings were evaluated as false positives. Network access is limited to the BrowserAct API endpoint. API key is retrieved from environment variables and used only for authentication. No malicious behavior confirmed.

medium_findings.title: External Command Documentation in SKILL.md
medium_findings.description: The SKILL.md file contains documentation for executing Python scripts with backtick formatting. This appears in code examples and is not actual command execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks are markdown formatting for code blocks. The documented command 'python -u ./scripts/amazon_best_selling_products_finder_api.py' is the intended skill usage pattern.

low_findings.title: HTTP Library Usage for API Calls
low_findings.description: The Python script uses the requests library to make HTTP calls to the BrowserAct API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: HTTP library calls are the core functionality of this API integration skill. All requests target the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The script retrieves the BrowserAct API key from the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API key retrieval pattern. The key is used only for Bearer token authentication to the BrowserAct API.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded BrowserAct API Endpoints
low_findings.description: The script contains hardcoded URLs for the BrowserAct API and Amazon marketplace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs point to legitimate, documented BrowserAct and Amazon domains. No malicious endpoints detected.

content.value_statement: Need to research Amazon products? This skill extracts structured data including titles, prices, ratings, reviews, and sales volume via the BrowserAct API.
actual_capabilities: Extract best-selling product data from Amazon by search keywords
Retrieve product titles, prices, star ratings, and review counts
Get past-month sales volume and promotional details
Access Amazon product badges like Amazon's Choice
Search across multiple Amazon regional marketplaces
Output structured data ready for analysis
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication
Rate limiting depends on BrowserAct service tier
Marketplace selection limited to supported Amazon regions

use_cases.title: Market Research Analyst
use_cases.description: Extract product listings and ratings to analyze market trends and identify opportunities for specific product categories.

use_cases.title: E-commerce Seller
use_cases.description: Monitor competitor pricing, sales volume, and promotional strategies to optimize your own product listings and pricing.

use_cases.title: Business Intelligence Specialist
use_cases.description: Compare product availability and pricing across different Amazon regional marketplaces to identify arbitrage opportunities.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Product Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find best-selling products on Amazon for keyword: {keyword}
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple request for top products in a category

prompt_templates.title: Detailed Product Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Get top 20 best-selling products from Amazon for {keyword} and show me the titles, prices, ratings, and review counts

prompt_templates.title: Regional Market Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare best-selling products for {keyword} across Amazon.com and Amazon.co.uk marketplaces

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract sales volume and promotional details for {keyword} on Amazon to analyze competitor performance
prompt_templates.scenario: Sales and promotion analysis for business decisions

output_examples.input: Find best-selling wireless headphones on Amazon
Get sales data for running shoes
output: Nike Air Zoom Pegasus - $129.99 - 4.5 stars (8,234 reviews) - 2,156 past month sales
Adidas Ultraboost 22 - $189.99 - 4.6 stars (5,678 reviews) - 1,892 past month sales

best_practices: Use specific keywords for more targeted product results
Check the BrowserAct API key configuration before running the skill
Monitor task status logs during execution for troubleshooting
anti_patterns: Avoid requesting extremely large product sets which may timeout
Do not skip API key verification before starting product extraction
Do not ignore rate limits when running multiple searches

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Go to https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations and create an account to get your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What Amazon marketplaces are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports multiple regional marketplaces including Amazon.com, Amazon.co.uk, Amazon.de, and more by specifying the marketplace_url parameter.

faq.question: How long does a product search take?
faq.answer: Execution time varies from 30 seconds to several minutes depending on the number of products requested and API response times.

faq.question: What data fields are included in the results?
faq.answer: Results include title, brand, prices, star rating, review count, past-month sales, availability, promotions, ASIN, category, badges, and product URL.

faq.question: Can I retry if a search fails?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes a retry mechanism. If the task fails without an authorization error, the agent will automatically retry once.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will detect invalid authorization and stop execution. You should verify your BrowserAct API key and provide a valid one.
